images.altText: Donald Trump speaking at a podium in the White House Press Briefing room.

articles.title: Deception campaign key to rescue of downed US fighter pilot from Iran

children.content: After an American fighter jet was shot down in a mountainous area of Iran, the US military pulled off an incredible rescue of two airmen, living up to its credo of "no man left behind", despite running into difficulty.

image.altText: black wreckage in an open landscape with mountains in the background

children.content: US President Donald Trump is holding a press conference with military officials at the White House to discuss the war in the Middle East. Follow live.

items.title: Trump praises pilot's rescue as Iran rejects 45-day ceasefire proposal

updates.title: Trump criticises Australia and other allies over lack of help
US president says rescue of F-15 service members 'very historic'

items.title: Artemis II breaks all-time distance record during historic loop of the Moon

children.content: The Artemis II astronauts are on track to break humanity's all-time distance record from Earth before embarking on a six-hour flyby of the Moon. Follow live.

image.altText: A male astronaut's head is seen in profile, looking back at Earth through a spacecraft cabin window.

items.title: Pro-climate son-in-law to Rupert Murdoch backs Farrer independent

children.content: Backers of Farrer independent candidate Michelle Milthorpe are celebrating after securing the support of a prominent pro-climate farmer who is also the son-in-law of media mogul Rupert Murdoch.

image.altText: Michelle Milthorpe talks to two people with their backs to the camera in the bistro of a pub.

items.title: A world without work. How do we survive the AI revolution?

children.content: The wave of change from artificial intelligence rolling across the corporate landscape is going largely unnoticed as attention understandably shifts to an escalating war.

image.altText: A silhouette of a person looking at a phone.

items.title: Israeli settler 'aggression' in the West bank 'is increasing'

children.content: Under the cover of regional conflict, Israelis who have established illegal settlements and outposts in the West Bank become emboldened to take over more land as world attention remains on the war in Iran.

image.altText: Mourners sit outside a hospital morgue in the West Bank.

items.title: How China uses ferries and fishing boats to pressure Taiwan

children.content: China quietly mobilised thousands of fishing boats twice in recent months to form floating barriers at least 300 kilometres long.

gallery.altText: A graphic features fishing vessels, a target and maps featuring the flags of China and Taiwan.

thumbnail.altText: A graphic features fishing vessels, a target and maps featuring the flags of China and Taiwan.

items.title: Chinese AI helping Iran target US forces with 'incredible precision'

children.content: Chinese satellite images enhanced by artificial intelligence could help Iran target US and allied forces to within a third of a square metre, military analysts say.

image.altText: An aerial view of land.

items.title: Fake job scams that may involve crimes target young people

children.content: Job seekers should be wary of sharing their bank account information with potential employers, as job scams targeting young people surge, according to Scamwatch.

image.altText: A man with a brown hoodie is scrolling on his phone.

items.title: Zein was haunted by what she had forgotten of Syria — until she embraced it

children.content: For Heywire winner Zein, her family is remembered in the fragments she remembers of Syria.

gallery.altText: A young woman seating in a garden.

thumbnail.altText: A young woman seating in a garden.

items.title: Australia's fuel shipments secured 'well into' May

children.content: Fuel shipments to Australia have been secured "well into" May, the energy minister says, as the number of service stations with diesel and petrol shortages fell over the Easter weekend.

image.altText: A close-up shot of Chris Bowen, looking serious and wearing a suit and tie.

items.title: Murray River ski boat launches into tree as race puts four in hospital

children.content: Flips, spin-outs and crashes have sent four competitors to hospital as the world's fastest water ski race was held without skiers for the second straight year after fatalities in the sport in 2024.

image.altText: Dozens of people on water ski boats at a river bank, with the Murray River in the background

items.title: The first world country in the grip of a poverty crisis

children.content: Once a mighty empire, Britain is now a broken kingdom. A staggering one fifth of the nation lives in poverty and that number is rising.

gallery.altText: A woman standing in a doorway with bins outside leaning against the wall.
Buses driving past the Houses of Parliament and Big Ben in London.
Narrow back street with a rubbish bin outside a home.

thumbnail.altText: A woman standing in a doorway with bins outside leaning against the wall.
Buses driving past the Houses of Parliament and Big Ben in London.
Narrow back street with a rubbish bin outside a home.

items.title: Outback tourist pub had two visitors this Easter amid fuel price pinch

children.content: Just two tourists have stopped at the iconic Pink Roadhouse during the Easter weekend, as travellers reconsidered their Easter weekend plans due to rising fuel costs.

image.altText: A road house sign at dusk

items.title: Commuters are switching from cars to e-bikes to save money on fuel

children.content: As high fuel prices continue to affect motorists, Australians are turning to e-bikes as an alternative form of transport, with retailers reporting a surge in sales.

image.altText: Sam smiles in a bike shop.

items.title: 'Zero profit': Many comedians pay thousands to put on festival shows

children.content: Doing a run of shows at the Melbourne International Comedy Festival is the pinnacle for a lot of Australian comedians, but the festival's tens of thousands of punters are rarely privy to the behind-the-scenes costs.

items.title: ABC Places quiz: Where in the world are we?

children.content: Today's ABC Places quiz features photos from 10 locations around the world. Your challenge is to guess where you are.

image.altText: A toy rabbit sits in a field of tulips

items.title: No Bluesfest? No worries as stars come out to wow Byron Easter crowds

children.content: There was no Bluesfest, but thousands of music fans still packed venues across the Byron Shire over the Easter long weekend.

image.altText: A concert with lots of people holding up their phones

items.title: Aussie stars testing the waters at Australian Open

children.content: Molly O'Callaghan and Kyle Chalmers claimed Australian Open gold medals in the 100m freestyle events, while Sam Short showed his 400m rivals a clean pair of heels on night one at the Gold Coast Aquatic Centre.

items.title: 'I hate points': Gunston's last-second behind helps Hawthorn claim thriller

children.content: Hawthorn's Jack Gunston kicks a behind in the dying seconds to defeat Geelong by one point in an Easter Monday classic.

image.altText: Jack Gunston hugs Mitch Lewis after Hawthorn's win

items.title: 'I don't know': Moses baffled as Eels denied in controversial Easter Monday classic

children.content: Wests Tigers five-eighth Jock Madden kicked the winning points, but Mitch Moses was left confused by a controversial call that went against the injury-ravaged Parramatta Eels.

image.altText: Mitch Moses talks to Peter Gough

items.title: The unexpected place new collectors are finding affordable art

children.content: Horse drawings and handcrafted ceramics from local artists and artisans are among the arts and crafts you can find at the Sydney Royal Easter Show.

gallery.altText: Sonja Bowden, a middle-aged blonde woman, smiles, holding a wine, beside a drawing of a dog, with a "First place" label.

thumbnail.altText: Sonja Bowden, a middle-aged blonde woman, smiles, holding a wine, beside a drawing of a dog, with a "First place" label.

items.title: The cleanest air on Earth holds a powerful lesson about our climate

children.content: Perched on Tasmania's wild north-west coast, Cape Grim has been quietly tracking the world's cleanest air for 50 years. Its data tell an interesting story about how our world has changed over half a century, including rare success stories.

image.altText: Graeme Pearman wears a red jacket and red flares, as he sets up a tower at Cape Grim in 1976

items.title: Bic Runga thought her first album in 15 years might never arrive

children.content: "There were definitely times where I thought I was never going to make anything again," the New Zealand star says of new album Red Sunset.

image.altText: Bic Runga standing outside an art studio at night, illuminated by colourful window displays.

items.title: How one extra tree too close to your house could cost you thousands

children.content: Tree canopy coverage is being boosted in every capital city, but one micro-level analysis has found a link between property prices and the proximity of trees to houses.

gallery.altText: Dark SUV driving down city street with lots of trees in it
A single-storey white house seen at a street level below multiple large trees with bushy green growth towering over it.

A single-storey white house seen at a street level below multiple large trees with bushy green growth towering over it.

items.title: Toddler dies after being pulled from water on Queensland property

children.content: An 18-month-old boy has died after being pulled from water at a private property on the Western Downs.

image.altText: Darling Downs Health sign in Tara

items.title: Angry customers who lost $10m in Zone RV collapse urge ASIC to investigate

children.content: With retirement dreams dashed, customers launch a nationwide campaign for the corporate watchdog to investigate the caravan company's former director over insolvent trading claims.

image.altText: retirees holding placards

items.title: UFOs over the oval: The sighting school kids were told not to talk about

children.content: Flying saucers, balloons or mass hysteria? It's 60 years today since Australia's biggest UFO sighting took place at a Melbourne school. Witnesses are still demanding answers about what happened that day.

items.title: Christian villages in Lebanon with no affiliation to Hezbollah attacked by Israel

children.content: Bashir was injured when Israel attacked his home in a Christian village in Lebanon's south, where many residents refuse to leave despite threats of destruction.

image.altText: A large cross and a flag of Lebanon erected on top of hill, underneath large letters read Rmeich

items.title: Price crunch has some servo owners asking: 'Is it really worth doing this?'

children.content: Workers are reporting an increase in abuse from customers as service station owners say they're not making money from fuel price rises.

image.altText: External shots of a 7/11 petrol station and fuel pump with an out of order sign.

items.title: Up to five on the run after smashing car into Brisbane luxury store

children.content: Police say a Mercedes SUV was deliberately driven into the store in the early hours today.

image.altText: Police tape in front of a store with a smashed in door.

items.title: Man found dead after being swept away in floodwaters in SA's far north

children.content: Police have found the body of a man in his 60s who was swept away trying to cross a flooded road on foot in SA's far north over the Easter weekend.

image.altText: A man looks into the camera. He has white hair and is wearing a hat.

items.title: A great gift or unexpected curse? Reflections on life as a new retiree

children.content: While time is retirement's great gift, it can become a curse for anyone who has devoted decades to a career and then suddenly wakes up with relevance deprivation syndrome and no firm plan on what comes next, writes Matt Eaton.

gallery.altText: Three men smile for a selfie. Two hold guitars while another holds up drumsticks while sat at a kit.
A man stands and looks back at the camera with Whitby Abbey in the background, green grass and blue sky.

items.title: Push to axe Kanye West from Wireless Festival after past antisemitic remarks

children.content: There is increasing pressure for the rapper to be pulled as headliner of the Wireless Festival in London, as UK PM Keir Starmer weighs in and Pepsi withdraws as lead sponsor.

image.altText: Kanye West performing on stage holding microphone in hand

items.title: Melbourne woman Eva Lasrini remembered as devoted mother of two

children.content: A Dandenong woman whose body was found south-west of Melbourne on Saturday has been remembered as a devoted mother who "deserved a life filled with safety, happiness, and peace".

image.altText: A woman smiles while holding a bouquet of flowers as her two adult daughters kiss her cheeks.

items.title: Artist shares origin story of Field of Light installation at Uluru

children.content: Bruce Munro's  art installation is inspired by Uluru. Ten years later, it remains one of the most popular attractions at the famous landmark.

gallery.altText: The camera looks over a mans shoulder onto the moonlit night scene of the Field of Lights glowing.
Vibrant reds, oranges and purples cover the ground under a night sky, while in the fore tourists take photos of each other.
The silhouette of Uluru hugs the horizon and patches of bright coloured light spreads out before it in a beautiful scene
